Mridul Rao
About Experience Writing

AI Engineer

Building real-time voice and agentic AI systems.

I am Mridul Rao, an AI engineer with a background in computer science and a strong interest in voice AI, LLM systems, and production-ready backend infrastructure. I enjoy building fast, reliable systems that make human-AI interaction more useful in real-world settings.

LinkedIn GitHub Medium
Read my story

About

What I work on

My work sits at the intersection of machine learning, telephony, scalable systems, and product-minded AI engineering.

Voice and real-time AI

I build real-time voice AI agents and end-to-end pipelines across SIP, speech-to-text, language models, and text-to-speech with a strong focus on latency, reliability, and user experience.

LLM systems

I work on agent architectures with function calling, retrieval, memory, observability, and production deployment patterns for small and large language models.

Engineering mindset

I like combining practical ML with strong backend engineering, whether that means GPU inference, system optimization, API design, or building infrastructure that holds up in production.

Background

A quick snapshot

Education

University of Southern California

I completed my M.S. in Computer Science at USC after building my foundation in computer science during my B.Tech. at Graphic Era University.

Experience

Data Science Mentor at Devtown

I mentored students through data science workshops and helped shape curriculum that stayed relevant to current industry needs.

Publication

Sentiment Analysis of User-Generated Data

I contributed to research presented at ICASIS 2023 on sentiment analysis using a CNN-BiLSTM approach, reflecting my long-running interest in applied machine learning and NLP.

Writing

Topics I write about

I regularly write to clarify my thinking and share practical lessons from building AI systems.

Voice AI

Telephony infrastructure for voice agents

I write about the systems behind modern voice agents, from call infrastructure to real-time communication patterns.

LLM Engineering

Deploying and optimizing model stacks

I share notes on attention efficiency, model deployment, inference tuning, and practical tradeoffs in production AI systems.

Agents

Observability, security, and RAG

I explore how reliable agent systems depend on the surrounding environment, guardrails, retrieval, and careful engineering.
